06 2005 2004 2003 2002 2001 2000 1999 1998 1997 1996 1995 1994 1993 1992 1991 1990 1989 1988 1987 1986 1985 1984 Skip to sidebar Dynamic-level scheduling for heterogeneous processor networks Gilbert Sih and Edward A. Lee Parallel and Distributed Proc

ih and Edward A. Lee Parallel and Distributed Processing , pp. 42-49, December 9-13, 1990. ABSTRACT Dynamic-level scheduling is an effective compile-time scheduling technique which accounts for interprocessor communication overhead when mapping prece

rocessor communication overhead when mapping precedence-constrained, communicating tasks onto arbitrarily interconnected processor networks. Scheduling and routing are performed simultaneously to account for limited interconnections between processor

unt for limited interconnections between processors, and communications are scheduled along with computations to eliminate shared-resource contention. The paper extends the dynamic-level scheduling methodology to encompass heterogeneous processing en
thodology to encompass heterogeneous processing environments, and presents two techniques designed to enhance scheduling performance: forward/backward scheduling, and precedence constraint appendage.
